expectations.text: Names exactly three long-lived branches: develop, uat (or staging), main
expectations.evidence: 'Long-lived branches' lists develop, staging, main (staging is acceptable substitute).

expectations.text: Specifies one-way promotion: develop to uat to main; explicitly forbids skipping a tier
expectations.evidence: Section 2 'No skipping' says 'a feature should never go directly from develop to main'.

expectations.text: Forbids backflow (main to uat as a normal flow); only post-hotfix forward-merge is allowed
expectations.evidence: 'No backflow: changes don't move from main back into staging or develop as a normal pattern. The only sanctioned reverse motion is the post-hotfix forward-merge'.

expectations.text: Includes a CI gate matrix where each tier has at least one gate the previous tier doesn't
expectations.evidence: Matrix shows integration/e2e optional on develop but required on staging; smoke tests in staging env required only at staging/main; smoke in prod env only at main.

expectations.text: Branch protection rules escalate with tier
expectations.evidence: Branch protection table: required approvers 1/1/2; restrict to admins only on main; linear history optional/yes/yes.

expectations.text: Source-ref enforcement is mentioned: PRs to uat must come from develop (or hotfix), PRs to main must come from uat
expectations.evidence: 'Restrict source branches' row: 'from develop or hotfix/* only' for staging, 'from staging only' for main; also mentions source-branch check CI gate.

expectations.text: Adoption sequence acknowledges this is a culture change and includes a transition step
expectations.evidence: Section 5 explicitly says 'This is a culture change, not just a config change' and lays out Week 0 through Week 4+ with branch creation from main tip and team communication.

expectations.text: Output does not cite this skill by name
expectations.evidence: No mention of 'branch-promotion-discipline' or 'this skill' anywhere.
